description: HTTP requests may include an optional top-level `id` for correlation and tracing.
When a request includes `id`, the server should echo the same `id` in the response envelope.
When a request omits `id`, the server should generate a response `id`.
Request correlation should not require an additional envelope; `id` remains a shallow optional field on the request itself.
The same request-correlation rule should apply to both the mock server and a production server so tests can rely on stable tracing behavior.
